Operation 'CreateTemporaryWorkPayrollPlacementDefinitionForUserMinimalV1' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks clarity on what is returned (output details) and what state changes occur (side effects). Added information about the return value (placement definition with ID) and clarified the legacy status more concisely for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Create a minimal definition for a new temporary work payroll placement.
data.current_description:
Create a definition for a new temporary work payroll placement. The minimal information must be provided, and a number of optional fields may be passed.

The user ID instead of the worker ID is used to identify the worker. The user must be a worker.

**This is a legacy endpoint**. [create-temporary-work-payroll-placement-definition-minimal-v1](#/Temporary%20work%20payroll%20placements%2C%20for%20hirers/CreateTemporaryWorkPayrollPlacementDefinitionMinimalV1) should be used instead. Only use this if your system does not know the worker ID.
data.description_suggestion: Create a definition for a new temporary work payroll placement using a worker's user ID. The minimal information must be provided, and optional fields may be included. The user must be a worker. Returns a placement definition object with the assigned placement ID. **This is a legacy endpoint** - use create-temporary-work-payroll-placement-definition-minimal-v1 instead if you have the worker ID.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on what is returned (output details) and what state changes occur (side effects). Added information about the return value (placement definition with ID) and clarified the legacy status more concisely for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Create a minimal definition for a new temporary work payroll placement.
data.current_description: Create a definition for a new temporary work payroll placement. The minimal information must be provided, and a number of optional fields may be passed.
data.description_suggestion: Create a definition for a new temporary work payroll placement using a worker ID. The minimal information must be provided, and optional fields may be included. Returns a placement definition object with the assigned placement ID that can be used for subsequent operations.
data.reasoning: Description is incomplete - it doesn't specify what is returned or what state changes occur. Added clarity about the return value (placement definition with ID) and its purpose for subsequent operations, improving AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'AddHirerLabelsToUserV1' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks clarity on return value and side effects. Added information about what is returned (updated worker profile) and clarified the idempotent behavior more explicitly. Improved legacy endpoint guidance for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Add 1 or more hirer labels to a worker.
data.current_description:
Add 1 or more of the labels that the hirer has defined for workers, to a worker, using the user ID instead of the worker ID. The user must be a worker.

The labels do not have to exist already. If the label is unknown, it will be created.

If a label has already been added to a worker, this will not result in an error.

**This is a legacy endpoint.** [add-hirer-labels-to-worker-v1](#/Worker%20profiles%2C%20for%20hirers/AddHirerLabelsToWorkerV1) should be used instead. Only use this if your system does not know the worker ID.
data.description_suggestion: Add one or more hirer-defined labels to a worker using their user ID. Unknown labels are automatically created. Idempotent - adding an existing label does not cause an error. Returns the updated worker profile with assigned labels. **This is a legacy endpoint** - use add-hirer-labels-to-worker-v1 if you have the worker ID.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on return value and side effects. Added information about what is returned (updated worker profile) and clarified the idempotent behavior more explicitly. Improved legacy endpoint guidance for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Remove 1 or more hirer labels from a worker.
data.current_description:
Remove 1 or more of the labels that the hirer has defined for workers, from a worker, using the user ID instead of the worker ID. The user must be a worker.

If the worker already does not have the label assigned to them, this will not result in an error.

**This is a legacy endpoint.** [remove-hirer-labels-from-worker-v1](#/Worker%20profiles%2C%20for%20hirers/RemoveHirerLabelsFromWorkerV1) should be used instead. Only use this if your system does not know the worker ID.
data.description_suggestion: Remove one or more hirer-defined labels from a worker using their user ID. Idempotent - removing a non-existent label does not cause an error. Returns the updated worker profile with remaining labels. **This is a legacy endpoint** - use remove-hirer-labels-from-worker-v1 if you have the worker ID.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on return value and side effects. Added information about what is returned (updated worker profile) and clarified the idempotent behavior more explicitly. Improved legacy endpoint guidance for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description:

If the worker already does not have the label assigned to them, this will not result in an error.

data.description_suggestion: Create a new worker profile in the Maqqie Platform. Accepts worker details via the request body and returns the created worker object with assigned identifier. This operation is part of the onboarding flow and establishes the worker record for subsequent operations such as framework agreement creation and shift assignments.
